north_star: The go-to benchmarking tool for investors and strategists: real-time-ish, beautiful, opinionated

data: Static CSVs / manual refresh — no live pipeline yet
Revenue forecast is naive trailing-growth extrapolation, no confidence intervals
Missing high-signal metrics: ROIC, FCF yield, net debt/EBITDA, NTM multiples
No peer-group or sub-industry granularity (e.g. 'Big Box' vs 'Grocery' within Consumer)
pipeline: No orchestration layer — monthly cadence is manual
SEC/EDGAR path exists but external API stub not wired
No data quality checks or anomaly flags (e.g. P/E outliers like KR at 60x)
product_surface: No interactivity — user can't filter, sort, or select peers
No historical multiple time series — only point-in-time snapshot
No export / download CTA for the tables

ideas: Add NTM (next-twelve-month) multiples alongside LTM — more actionable for investors
Surface net debt / EBITDA as a leverage signal per company
Add FCF margin and FCF yield — P/E is noisy, FCF is cleaner
Flag statistical outliers inline (e.g. red badge on P/E > 3 std devs from sector median)
Compute sector medians and quartiles so users see where each name sits in the distribution
Replace naive trailing-growth with consensus-anchored estimates (FactSet / Bloomberg API when credentialed)
Add a bear / base / bull scenario band on the revenue chart
Show CAGR implied by forecast vs. historical CAGR — instant sanity check
Add Tech, Healthcare, Energy, Real Estate — users expect full market coverage
Introduce sub-industry groupings (e.g. within Tech: Semiconductors, SaaS, Hardware)
Allow custom peer group construction: user picks 5 tickers, gets instant comp table
Wrap run_comparables.py in a lightweight scheduler (GitHub Actions cron on first weekday of month)
Add data validation step: schema checks, null-rate thresholds, range guards before writing output
Version outputs with YYYY-MM suffix AND maintain a 'latest' symlink so the Quarto page always reads current
Emit a pipeline run log (run_log.json) with timestamp, rows written, error count — useful for debugging
Make the Quarto page interactive: Observable JS or a lightweight React embed for sortable, filterable tables
Add sparklines for revenue trend inline in the table — visual > numbers for quick scanning
One-click CSV / Excel download per industry table
'Last updated' badge prominent at top with a tooltip explaining data lag
Sector summary card at top of each section: median EV/EBITDA, median rev growth, # of names — fast orientation
Auto-generate a 2-3 sentence 'sector pulse' blurb per industry using an LLM call over the freshly computed metrics
Flag month-over-month multiple expansion / compression with directional arrows
Add a 'notable movers' section at top of page — top 3 names with biggest multiple change this month
Email digest: monthly summary table sent to subscribers on pipeline run day
Embeddable widget: iframe-able comp table for external blogs / newsletters
API endpoint: serve the comparables data as JSON so power users can pull programmatically

launch_checklist_done: Wire run_comparables.py end-to-end for Consumer + Financials + Industrials
Build comparables.qmd with embedded tables reading from those CSVs
Sector median row added to each industry table
north_star_metric: Monthly active sessions on comparables page + CSV download rate — if analysts are downloading, the data is good enough to trust
llm_reasoning_prompt: When suggesting improvements, always ask: does this help an investor make a faster, better-informed judgment about relative value? If yes, prioritize. If it only adds visual polish without analytical lift, deprioritize.
